title: Calea lobata
taxonomy: Calea lobata is classified within the family Asteraceae, which includes a diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This family is further divided into various tribes and subfamilies based on morphological and genetic characteristics. Calea lobata belongs to the tribe Heliantheae, which is a large and widespread group of daisy-like plants. Within the tribe Heliantheae, Calea lobata is placed in the subfamily Asteroideae, a group that is known for its composite flowers and achenes. The specific classification of Calea lobata can be represented as follows: Calea lobata (family Asteraceae, tribe Heliantheae, subfamily Asteroideae).
uses: Calea lobata is a plant traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine. Its medicinal uses include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is also used to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Additionally, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. Research suggests that Calea lobata may also have antioxidant effects.

intro: Calea lobata is a perennial plant native to Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The plant is traditionally used in Mexican herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Calea lobata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calea lobata are teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
